 {"id":"caam-ffz1","title":"E2E: Complete backup-activate-switch workflow","description":"# E2E Test: Complete Backup-Activate-Switch Workflow

## Goal
Test the complete user workflow from backup through activation and switching.

## Workflow Steps

### 1. Initial Setup
- Create mock auth files for multiple providers
- Initialize vault

### 2. Backup Phase
```
backup codex account1@example.com
backup codex account2@example.com
backup claude personal@example.com
```

### 3. Activation Phase
```
activate codex account1@example.com
verify auth files match account1
activate codex account2@example.com
verify auth files match account2
```

### 4. Switch Phase
```
status codex  # shows account2
activate codex account1@example.com
status codex  # shows account1
```

### 5. Auto-Backup Verification
- Verify _original profile created on first activate
- Verify auto-backup timestamps

## Test File
internal/e2e/workflows/backup_activate_test.go

## Logging Requirements
- Log each step with timestamps
- Log auth file contents (redacted)
- Log vault state after each operation
- Duration metrics for each operation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full workflow completes without error
- Auth files correctly switched
- Vault state consistent
- Detailed logs for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:06:24.608763064-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T20:38:30.736156003-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:38:30.736156003-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented complete E2E backup-activate-switch workflow tests using ExtendedHarness. Includes: TestE2E_CompleteBackupActivateSwitchWorkflow (5 phases), TestE2E_RapidProfileSwitching (performance test with sub-100ms verification), TestE2E_CrossProviderSwitching (provider isolation). All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ffz1","depends_on_id":"caam-ldjp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:12:38.492164884-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-fhrk","title":"TUI: Add post-mutation state refresh and error handling","description":"After any mutation (activate, delete, refresh), TUI state must be refreshed.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create refreshProfiles() method that reloads all profiles from vault
2. Call after every successful mutation
3. Preserve selected index if possible (may need to adjust if profile deleted)
4. Create showError() helper for consistent error display

ERROR HANDLING PATTERNS:
- File not found: 'Profile not found in vault'
- Permission denied: 'Cannot write to auth file - check permissions'
- Vault corrupted: 'Profile data corrupted - try re-backup'

SUCCESS MESSAGES:
- Activate: 'Activated {profile} for {provider}'
- Delete: 'Deleted {profile}'
- Refresh: 'Refreshed {profile} - new token valid until {date}'

-{"id":"caam-fj9a","title":"Integrate identity extraction into Profile loading","description":"# Task: Integrate identity extraction into Profile loading

## Overview
Automatically extract and attach identity information when loading profiles, so users see rich identity data in commands like `caam ls` and `caam status`.

## Technical Details

### Profile Enhancement
```go
// internal/profile/profile.go

type Profile struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // NEW: Identity information extracted from auth files
    Identity *identity.Identity `json:"identity,omitempty"`
}

// LoadWithIdentity loads profile and extracts identity
func (p *Profile) LoadWithIdentity() error {
    // 1. Load profile as normal
    // 2. Determine provider
    // 3. Call appropriate identity extractor
    // 4. Attach to profile
}
```

### Display Enhancement
```
$ caam ls claude

PROFILES FOR CLAUDE:
  NAME               EMAIL                 PLAN      STATUS
  alice              alice@gmail.com       Max       Active
  bob                bob@company.com       Team      Ready
  work-account       unknown               unknown   Ready
```

### Rotation Enhancement
Enterprise/Team accounts may have different limits - use identity.PlanType in rotation scoring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identity extracted on profile load (lazy, cached)
- [ ] `caam ls` shows email and plan type
- [ ] `caam status` shows full identity details
- [ ] Rotation considers plan type in scoring
- [ ] Graceful fallback when identity unavailable

## Dependencies
- Implement JWT parser for Codex (caam-vek1)
- Implement Claude identity parser (caam-j6rg)
- Implement Gemini identity parser (caam-v021)

## Files to Modify
- internal/profile/profile.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/ls.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/status.go
- internal/rotation/scoring.go

 {"id":"caam-fu2w","title":"EPIC: Live Usage Monitor & Alerts","description":"# EPIC: Live Usage Monitor

## Strategic Context

When managing multiple AI accounts, users want real-time visibility into the status of all accounts. This is different from the TUI (which focuses on profile management) - this is a MONITORING dashboard that shows live rate limit status, refresh continuously, and alerts when any account needs attention.

## Use Cases

1. **Background Monitoring**: Keep a terminal window showing all account status
2. **tmux/Screen Integration**: Show status in status bar
3. **Alert Notifications**: Desktop notifications when limits approaching
4. **Scripting Integration**: Machine-readable status for custom workflows

## User Experience

### Interactive Monitor
```
$ caam monitor

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LIVE USAGE MONITOR                                  ║
║                    Last updated: 12:34:56                              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║                                                                         ║
║  CLAUDE                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  alice@gmail.com  🟢 ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42% │ resets 4h 12m │ ready  ║
║  bob@gmail.com    🟡 ██████████████░░░░░░ 71% │ resets 1h 45m │ ready  ║
║  carol@gmail.com  🔴 ████████████████████ 95% │ resets 0h 23m │ cooldown║
║                                                                         ║
║  CODEX                                                                  ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  work@company.com 🟢 ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 18% │ resets 3h 56m │ ready  ║
║                                                                         ║
║  GEMINI                                                                 ║
║  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  ║
║  (no profiles configured)                                               ║
║                                                                         ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
  Refresh: 30s │ [r] refresh now │ [p] precheck │ [q] quit
```

### tmux Integration
```bash
# In .tmux.conf
set -g status-right '#(caam monitor --format brief)'

# Shows: claude:42%▲ codex:18%▲
```

### Alert Mode
```
$ caam monitor --alert --threshold 80

[2024-01-10 12:34:56] ⚠️  claude/carol@gmail.com at 85%
[2024-01-10 12:35:12] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 90%
[2024-01-10 12:35:45] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 95% - SWITCH RECOMMENDED
```

## Implementation

### Monitor State
```go
// internal/monitor/monitor.go

type Monitor struct {
    interval   time.Duration
    providers  []string
    
    fetcher    *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    vault      *authfile.Vault
    healthStore *health.Storage
    db         *db.DB
    
    state      *MonitorState
    mu         sync.RWMutex
}

type MonitorState struct {
    Profiles  map[string]*ProfileState  // "provider/name" -> state
    UpdatedAt time.Time
    Errors    []string
}

type ProfileState struct {
    Provider     string
    ProfileName  string
    Usage        *usage.UsageInfo
    Health       health.HealthStatus
    PoolStatus   authpool.PoolStatus
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownUntil *time.Time
    Alert        *Alert
}

type Alert struct {
    Type    AlertType
    Message string
    Since   time.Time
}
```

### Output Renderers
```go
// internal/monitor/render.go

type Renderer interface {
    Render(state *MonitorState) string
}

type TableRenderer struct {}    // Rich ASCII table
type BriefRenderer struct {}    // One-line summary
type JSONRenderer struct {}     // Machine-readable
type AlertRenderer struct {}    // Alerts only
```

### Alert System
```go
// internal/monitor/alerts.go

type AlertManager struct {
    threshold   float64
    notifier    Notifier
    history     []*Alert
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(alert *Alert) error
}

type DesktopNotifier struct {}  // libnotify / osascript
type TerminalNotifier struct {} // Print to stderr
type WebhookNotifier struct {}  // POST to URL
```

### Background Polling
```go
func (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(m.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return nil
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.refresh(ctx)
        }
    }
}

func (m *Monitor) refresh(ctx context.Context) {
    // 1. List all profiles from vault
    // 2. Fetch usage for each (concurrent)
    // 3. Check health status
    // 4. Check cooldown status
    // 5. Generate alerts
    // 6. Update state
}
```

## Commands

### caam monitor
```
Usage:
  caam monitor [flags]

Flags:
  -i, --interval duration   Refresh interval (default 30s)
  -p, --provider strings    Providers to monitor (default: all)
  -f, --format string       Output format: table, brief, json, alerts
  -a, --alert               Enable alert mode (log alerts to stderr)
  -t, --threshold float     Alert threshold (default 0.8)
  -n, --notify              Enable desktop notifications
  -w, --webhook string      Webhook URL for alerts
  -1, --once                Fetch once and exit (no live monitoring)
```

### Examples
```bash
# Interactive monitor
caam monitor

# tmux status bar integration
caam monitor --format brief --once

# Alert mode with desktop notifications
caam monitor --alert --notify --threshold 80

# JSON output for scripting
caam monitor --format json --once | jq '.profiles[] | select(.usage.primary_percent > 80)'

# Monitor specific provider
caam monitor --provider claude
```

## Integration Points

### TUI Integration
Add monitor panel to existing TUI (internal/tui/):
- Real-time usage bars for each profile
- Alert indicators
- Quick switch actions

### Daemon Integration  
Monitor can run as part of daemon for:
- Background alert generation
- Desktop notifications
- Webhook triggers

## Deliverables
1. Monitor struct and state management
2. Background polling loop
3. Table renderer (rich ASCII)
4. Brief renderer (one-line)
5. JSON renderer
6. Alert system with thresholds
7. Desktop notification integration
8. Webhook notification
9. caam monitor command
10. TUI integration (optional)

## Dependencies
- internal/usage/ (for fetching rate limits)
- internal/health/ (for health status)
- internal/db/ (for cooldown status)
- EPIC: Background Auth Pool Daemon (for pool status)

## Enables
- Real-time visibility into account status
- Proactive notifications before limits hit
- tmux/screen integration for always-visible status
- Scripting and automation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:16:21.51807078-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T00:16:21.51807078-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fu2w","depends_on_id":"caam-3qdv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:48.810497527-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-fwl","title":"SQLite database setup and migrations","description":"## Purpose
Set up SQLite database infrastructure for usage analytics and activity tracking.

## Background
SQLite was chosen over BoltDB (used by codex-pool) for its query flexibility.
We need a migration system to handle schema evolution as features are added.

## Technical Requirements

### Database Location
```
~/.caam/data/caam.db
```

### Migration System
```go
// internal/db/migrations.go
type Migration struct {
    Version int
    Name    string
    Up      string
    Down    string
}

var migrations = []Migration{
    {
        Version: 1,
        Name:    "initial_schema",
        Up: `
            CREATE TABLE schema_version (
                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                applied_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE activity_log (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
                event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                details TEXT,
                duration_seconds INTEGER
            );
            
            CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
                provider TEXT NOT NULL,
                profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
                total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
                last_activated DATETIME,
                last_error DATETIME,
                PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
            );
            
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_timestamp ON activity_log(timestamp);
            CREATE INDEX idx_activity_provider ON activity_log(provider, profile_name);
        `,
    },
}

func RunMigrations(db *sql.DB) error {
    // Apply pending migrations
}
```

### Database Manager
```go
// internal/db/db.go
type DB struct {
    conn *sql.DB
}

func Open() (*DB, error) {
    // Ensure directory exists
    // Open connection with WAL mode
    // Run migrations
}

func (d *DB) Close() error
```

## Dependencies
- Uses modernc.org/sqlite (CGO-free driver)
- Directory utilities from existing caam code

## Files to Create
- internal/db/db.go
- internal/db/migrations.go
- internal/db/db_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database created automatically on first use
- [ ] Migrations run idempotently
- [ ] WAL mode enabled for concurrent reads
- [ ] Tests verify migration system works
- [ ] Graceful handling of corrupt database","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:11.418686244-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T19:57:08.742627263-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:57:08.742627263-05:00","close_reason":"Added SQLite DB infrastructure with migrations, WAL mode, and corruption recovery.","comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting SQLite DB + migrations: add internal/db with Open (WAL mode) + migration runner + tests (including corrupt DB handling). Will use modernc.org/sqlite per spec.","created_at":"2025-12-18T00:52:57Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"caam-fwl","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented internal/db with modernc.org/sqlite: Open/OpenAt creates ~/.caam/data/caam.db (or CAAM_HOME), enables WAL + foreign_keys, runs idempotent migrations, and auto-renames corrupt DB files before recreating.  {"id":"caam-fwz","title":"OAuth refresh handler for Claude Code","description":"# OAuth Refresh Handler for Claude Code

## Purpose
Implement OAuth token refresh for Claude Code to automatically renew tokens before expiry.

## Research Required
**IMPORTANT**: Claude Code OAuth implementation needs reverse engineering.

### Questions to Answer
1. Where is the oauth.json file located?
2. What is the token endpoint URL?
3. What is the client_id?
4. Is it JSON body or form-encoded?
5. What scopes are required?

### Likely Locations to Check
- `~/.claude/oauth.json`
- `~/.claude.ai/oauth.json`  
- `~/.config/claude/oauth.json`
- Check Claude Code source/docs if available

## Technical Requirements (Template)

### Refresh Function
```go
func RefreshClaudeToken(ctx context.Context, refreshToken string) (*TokenResponse, error) {
    // Build request
    body := map[string]string{
        "client_id":     claudeClientID,
        "grant_type":    "refresh_token",
        "refresh_token": refreshToken,
        // "scope":      "...",  // if needed
    }
    
    req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "POST", claudeTokenURL, ...)
    req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")  // or form-encoded
    
    // Execute
    resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("refresh request failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer resp.Body.Close()
    
    // Parse response
    if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
        // Handle error
    }
    
    var tokenResp TokenResponse
    json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&tokenResp)
    
    return &tokenResp, nil
}
```

### TokenResponse Structure
```go
type TokenResponse struct {
    AccessToken  string `json:"access_token"`
    RefreshToken string `json:"refresh_token,omitempty"` // May rotate
    ExpiresIn    int    `json:"expires_in"`              // Seconds
    ExpiresAt    string `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`    // ISO8601
    TokenType    string `json:"token_type"`
}
```

### File Update
```go
func UpdateClaudeAuth(path string, resp *TokenResponse) error {
    // Read existing file
    existing, _ := os.ReadFile(path)
    var auth map[string]any
    json.Unmarshal(existing, &auth)
    
    // Update fields
    auth["access_token"] = resp.AccessToken
    if resp.RefreshToken != "" {
        auth["refresh_token"] = resp.RefreshToken
    }
    auth["expires_at"] = resp.ExpiresAt
    // ... or calculate from expires_in
    
    // Atomic write
    return atomicWriteJSON(path, auth)
}
```

## Safety Requirements
1. **Never lose refresh token** - if refresh fails, keep original
2. **Atomic writes** - use temp file + rename
3. **Backup original** - keep .bak file before overwrite
4. **Verify new token** - optionally test before committing

## Error Handling
- Invalid refresh token → mark profile as "needs login"
- Network error → retry with backoff
- Rate limited → respect Retry-After header
- Unknown error → log and keep original token

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Research: Document Claude Code OAuth endpoints
- [ ] RefreshClaudeToken function implemented
- [ ] UpdateClaudeAuth with atomic writes
- [ ] Unit tests with mock HTTP server
- [ ] Integration test with real credentials (optional, manual)
- [ ] Error handling for all failure modes

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/refresh/claude.go` (new)
- `internal/refresh/claude_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health metadata storage (for updating expiry)
- Token expiry parsing (for reading current state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:20:23.871008574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:35:02.534599402-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OAuth refresh logic for Claude Code. Includes RefreshClaudeToken and UpdateClaudeAuth with atomic writes. Endpoint and Client ID are placeholders needing verification.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-fwz","depends_on_id":"caam-thb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:27:54.952222175-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"caam-fwz","author":"ubuntu","text":"[ubuntu at 2025-12-18]
Follow-up from caam-dt4: added token endpoint allowlist guard (prevents refresh tokens being posted to non-provider hosts except loopback for tests) and updated CLI to treat refresh unsupported as skipped. Claude endpoint/client_id still best-guess placeholders; needs verification if we want guaranteed Claude refresh support.","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:32:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"caam-nmm","title":"Implement profiles table panel (center)","description":"## Task
Create the center panel showing profiles for the selected provider as a table.

## Columns
- Name (profile name)
- Auth Mode (oauth, api-key)
- Status (logged in ✓/✗, locked 🔒)
- Last Used (relative time)
- Account (label if set)

## Implementation
- Use bubbles/table component
- Filter by active provider
- Up/down navigation
- Sorting by last used (most recent first)

## Visual Example
```
┌─ Codex Profiles ───────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Name          Auth    Status      Last Used   Account  │
│ ▶ work-1      oauth   ✓           2h ago      jeff@... │
│   work-2      oauth   ✓           1d ago      bob@...  │
│   personal    api-key ✓ 🔒        now         -        │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

 {"id":"caam-npy","title":"Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement token freshness comparison and sync algorithm

## Context
The core logic that determines which token is "fresher" and should be synchronized. This is the brain of the sync system.

## Token Freshness

### What Makes a Token Fresh?
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. A "fresher" token:
1. Has a later expiry time (primary criterion)
2. Was created/modified more recently (tiebreaker)

### Token Structure Analysis

#### Claude Token (`.claude.json`)
```json
{
  "oauthToken": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauthToken.expiry`

#### Codex Token (`auth.json`)
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "refresh_token": "...",
  "expires_at": 1734623340
}
```
Key field: `expires_at` (Unix timestamp)

#### Gemini Token (`settings.json`)
```json
{
  "oauth_credentials": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "expiry": "2025-12-20T14:29:00Z"
  }
}
```
Key field: `oauth_credentials.expiry`

### Freshness Extractor
```go
package sync

// TokenFreshness represents the freshness of a token
type TokenFreshness struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    ModifiedAt  time.Time
    IsExpired   bool
    Source      string // "local" or machine name
}

// ExtractFreshness parses auth files to determine token freshness
func ExtractFreshness(provider, profile string, authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)

// CompareFreshness returns true if a is fresher than b
func CompareFreshness(a, b *TokenFreshness) bool {
    // Primary: later expiry wins
    if !a.ExpiresAt.Equal(b.ExpiresAt) {
        return a.ExpiresAt.After(b.ExpiresAt)
    }
    // Tiebreaker: later modification wins
    return a.ModifiedAt.After(b.ModifiedAt)
}
```

### Provider-Specific Extractors
```go
type FreshnessExtractor interface {
    Extract(authFiles map[string][]byte) (*TokenFreshness, error)
}

func GetExtractor(provider string) FreshnessExtractor {
    switch provider {
    case "claude":
        return &ClaudeFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "codex":
        return &CodexFreshnessExtractor{}
    case "gemini":
        return &GeminiFreshnessExtractor{}
    }
}
```

## Sync Algorithm

### Sync Operation
```go
type SyncOperation struct {
    Provider    string
    Profile     string
    Direction   string    // "push" or "pull"
    Machine     *Machine
    LocalFresh  *TokenFreshness
    RemoteFresh *TokenFreshness
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Operation   *SyncOperation
    Success     bool
    BytesSent   int64
    BytesRecv   int64
    Duration    time.Duration
    Error       error
}
```

### Full Sync Algorithm
```go
func (s *Syncer) SyncWithMachine(m *Machine) ([]*SyncResult, error) {
    results := []*SyncResult{}
    
    // 1. Connect to remote
    client, err := s.pool.Get(m)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("connection failed: %w", err)
    }
    defer s.pool.Release(m.ID)
    
    // 2. Get local profiles
    localProfiles := s.vault.ListAllProfiles()
    
    // 3. Get remote profiles
    remoteProfiles, err := s.listRemoteProfiles(client)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("list remote profiles: %w", err)
    }
    
    // 4. Merge profile lists (union)
    allProfiles := mergeProfileLists(localProfiles, remoteProfiles)
    
    // 5. For each profile, compare and sync
    for _, p := range allProfiles {
        op := s.determineSyncOperation(client, p)
        if op == nil {
            continue // Already in sync
        }
        
        result := s.executeOperation(client, op)
        results = append(results, result)
    }
    
    return results, nil
}
```

### Determine Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) determineSyncOperation(client *SSHClient, p ProfileRef) *SyncOperation {
    localFresh := s.getLocalFreshness(p)
    remoteFresh := s.getRemoteFreshness(client, p)
    
    switch {
    case localFresh == nil && remoteFresh != nil:
        // Only exists on remote: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    case localFresh != nil && remoteFresh == nil:
        // Only exists locally: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(localFresh, remoteFresh):
        // Local is fresher: push
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "push", ...}
        
    case CompareFreshness(remoteFresh, localFresh):
        // Remote is fresher: pull
        return &SyncOperation{Direction: "pull", ...}
        
    default:
        // Equal freshness: no action
        return nil
    }
}
```

### Execute Sync Operation
```go
func (s *Syncer) executeOperation(client *SSHClient, op *SyncOperation) *SyncResult {
    start := time.Now()
    
    switch op.Direction {
    case "push":
        // Read local files
        files, err := s.vault.ReadProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to remote
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        err = client.BatchWrite(remotePathFiles(remotePath, files))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
    case "pull":
        // Read remote files
        remotePath := s.remoteProfilePath(client.machine, op.Provider, op.Profile)
        files, err := client.BatchRead(remoteFilePaths(remotePath))
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
        
        // Write to local vault
        err = s.vault.WriteProfileFiles(op.Provider, op.Profile, files)
        if err != nil {
            return &SyncResult{Error: err, ...}
        }
    }
    
    return &SyncResult{
        Success:  true,
        Duration: time.Since(start),
        ...
    }
}
```

## Conflict Resolution

### Simultaneous Updates
If two machines update the same profile at nearly the same time:
- Both will detect their local as "fresher"
- Both will push
- Result: last write wins (deterministic by timestamp)

This is acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: conflict detection and resolution UI.

### Expired Tokens
Even expired tokens might be useful (for refresh_token). Don't skip expired tokens in sync.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Claude tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Codex tokens
- [ ] Freshness extraction for Gemini tokens
- [ ] Freshness comparison logic correct
- [ ] Push operation works
- [ ] Pull operation works
- [ ] Union merge of profile lists works
- [ ] Full sync algorithm correct
- [ ] Conflict handling deterministic
- [ ] Unit tests for freshness extraction
- [ ] Unit tests for comparison logic
- [ ] Integration test for full sync

## Files to Create
- `internal/sync/freshness.go`
- `internal/sync/freshness_test.go`
- `internal/sync/algorithm.go`
- `internal/sync/algorithm_test.go`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-683 (SSH transport)
- Part of epic: caam-2bm (Multi-Machine Vault Sync)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:47.976206972-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T16:46:32.641981054-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T16:46:32.641981054-05:00","close_reason":"Sync algorithm fully implemented: algorithm.go with Syncer, SyncWithMachine, SyncProfile, SyncAll, push/pull operations; freshness.go with Claude/Codex/Gemini extractors; all tests passing in algorithm_test.go and freshness_test.go.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-npy","depends_on_id":"caam-3wx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:07.96014303-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-npy","depends_on_id":"caam-683","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:13.067702381-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"caam-ogpo","title":"Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths","description":"# Task: Performance benchmarks for critical sub-100ms paths

## Overview
Benchmark tests to ensure critical operations meet the sub-100ms latency target that makes caam feel instant.

## Why This Matters
caam differentiates by being FAST:
- Profile switching: <50ms (not 30 seconds with browser)
- Auth file swap: <10ms
- Rate limit detection: <1ms per line
- PTY injection: <5ms

If these are slow, caam loses its value proposition.

## Benchmarks

### 1. Auth File Swap
```go
func BenchmarkAuthFileSwap(b *testing.B) {
    vault := authfile.NewTestVault(b)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "alice", largeAuthData)
    vault.CreateProfile("claude", "bob", largeAuthData)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        profile := []string{"alice", "bob"}[i%2]
        vault.SwapActive(profile)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per swap
    // Report: b.ReportMetric(float64(elapsed)/float64(b.N), "ns/swap")
}
```

### 2. Rate Limit Detection
```go
func BenchmarkRateLimitDetection(b *testing.B) {
    detector := ratelimit.NewDetector(ratelimit.DefaultPatterns())
    lines := loadTestLines(1000) // Mix of normal and rate limit lines
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        for _, line := range lines {
            detector.Check(line)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per 1000 lines
}
```

### 3. PTY Command Injection
```go
func BenchmarkPTYInjection(b *testing.B) {
    ctrl := pty.NewTestController(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        ctrl.InjectCommand("/login")
    }
    
    // Target: <5ms per injection
}
```

### 4. JWT Parsing
```go
func BenchmarkJWTParsing(b *testing.B) {
    token := loadTestJWT() // Large JWT with many claims
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        identity.ExtractFromJWT(token)
    }
    
    // Target: <1ms per parse
}
```

### 5. Log Parsing (per file)
```go
func BenchmarkLogParsing(b *testing.B) {
    logFile := createTestLogFile(10000) // 10k entries
    scanner := logs.NewClaudeScanner()
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        scanner.ScanFile(ctx, logFile, time.Time{})
    }
    
    // Target: <100ms per 10k entries
}
```

### 6. Burn Rate Calculation
```go
func BenchmarkBurnRateCalc(b *testing.B) {
    entries := generateTestEntries(1000)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        usage.CalculateBurnRate(entries, 2*time.Hour)
    }
    
    // Target: <10ms per calculation
}
```

### 7. Full Handoff Latency (E2E)
```go
func BenchmarkHandoffLatency(b *testing.B) {
    runner := setupTestRunner(b)
    
    b.ResetTimer()
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        start := time.Now()
        runner.SimulateRateLimitAndHandoff()
        elapsed := time.Since(start)
        
        if elapsed > 5*time.Second {
            b.Errorf("Handoff too slow: %v", elapsed)
        }
    }
    
    // Target: <5 seconds total (including login wait)
}
```

## Performance Targets Summary
| Operation | Target | Critical |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Auth file swap | <10ms | Yes |
| Rate limit check (per line) | <0.1ms | Yes |
| PTY injection | <5ms | Yes |
| JWT parsing | <1ms | No |
| Log file parse (10k lines) | <100ms | No |
| Burn rate calculation | <10ms | No |
| Full handoff (E2E) | <5s | Yes |

## Reporting
- Run benchmarks in CI
- Track regression over time
- Alert if critical paths exceed targets
- Generate flamegraphs for slow operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All critical paths benchmarked
- [ ] Tests pass on CI
- [ ] Results logged with clear metrics
- [ ] No critical path exceeds target
- [ ] Flamegraph generation for profiling

## Files to Create
- internal/authfile/vault_bench_test.go
- internal/ratelimit/detector_bench_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_bench_test.go
- internal/identity/jwt_bench_test.go
- internal/logs/scanner_bench_test.go
- internal/usage/burnrate_bench_test.go
- internal/exec/handoff_bench_test.go

 {"id":"caam-ojjb","title":"Unit tests for internal/pty controller (cross-platform)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/pty Controller (Cross-Platform)

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the PTY controller including command injection, output monitoring, and cross-platform behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. PTY Creation Tests
```go
func TestPTYCreation(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Creating PTY for simple command")
    
    cmd := exec.Command("cat")
    ctrl, err := pty.NewController(cmd)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Creation result: err=%v", err)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Verifying PTY is functional")
    // Write test input
    ctrl.InjectCommand("hello")
    
    // Read output
    output, err := ctrl.ReadWithTimeout(time.Second)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Read output: %q, err=%v", output, err)
    
    require.Contains(t, output, "hello")
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Closing PTY")
    ctrl.Close()
}
```

### 2. Command Injection Tests
- Inject single command
- Inject command with special characters
- Inject multiple commands rapidly
- Inject while output is streaming

### 3. Pattern Waiting Tests
```go
func TestWaitForPattern(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        pattern  string
        output   string
        timeout  time.Duration
        wantErr  bool
    }{
        {
            name:    "pattern found",
            pattern: "SUCCESS",
            output:  "Processing...\nSUCCESS: done",
            timeout: time.Second,
            wantErr: false,
        },
        {
            name:    "pattern not found - timeout",
            pattern: "SUCCESS",
            output:  "ERROR: failed",
            timeout: 100 * time.Millisecond,
            wantErr: true,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] Pattern: %q", tc.pattern)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Expected output: %q", tc.output)
            t.Logf("[TEST] Timeout: %v", tc.timeout)
            
            // ... test execution ...
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Result: err=%v, wantErr=%v", err, tc.wantErr)
        })
    }
}
```

### 4. Platform-Specific Tests
```go
func TestPlatformSupport(t *testing.T) {
    t.Logf("[TEST] Running on: %s/%s", runtime.GOOS, runtime.GOARCH)
    
    switch runtime.GOOS {
    case "linux", "darwin":
        t.Log("[TEST] Unix PTY support expected")
        testUnixPTY(t)
    case "windows":
        t.Log("[TEST] Windows ConPTY support (or graceful error)")
        testWindowsPTY(t)
    default:
        t.Skipf("[TEST] Unsupported platform: %s", runtime.GOOS)
    }
}
```

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- PTY creation with invalid command
- Read from closed PTY
- Write to closed PTY
- Timeout handling

### 6. Resource Cleanup Tests
- Verify PTY is closed on context cancel
- Verify no file descriptor leaks
- Verify child process is terminated

## Logging Requirements
- Log platform/OS at start
- Log PTY file descriptors
- Log all injected commands
- Log all read output
- Log timing of operations

## Cross-Platform Notes
- Linux/macOS: Uses unix.Openpty or github.com/creack/pty
- Windows: Uses ConPTY or gracefully errors with instructions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PTY creation works on Linux and macOS
- [ ] Command injection is reliable
- [ ] Pattern waiting has proper timeout
- [ ] Resource cleanup is verified
- [ ] Windows either works or errors gracefully
- [ ] No flaky tests

## Files to Create
- internal/pty/controller_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_unix_test.go
- internal/pty/controller_windows_test.go
